chapter_title.ar: باب مَنِ اضْطُرَّ إِلَى الْخَمْرِ لِلدَّوَاءِ أَوْ لِلْعَطَشِ أَوْ لِلتَّقِيَّةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – The one who is desperate to the wine for the medication, or for the thirst, or for the dissimulation

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل ومجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/274)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل ومجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/274)
text: مَنِ اكْتَحَلَ بِمِيلٍ مِنْ مُسْكِرٍ كَحَلَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِمِيلٍ مِنْ نَارٍ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Marwak Bin Ubeyd, from a man,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who applies (in the eyes) with a needle from an intoxicant, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would Apply (in his eyes) a needle from Fire’.
en.sarwar: 7. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Marwak ibn ‘Ubayd from a man who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one uses intoxicating things as eye medicine with an applicator of medicine to the eye Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, will apply as such with an applicator of fire.’”
